title: S² Works
html:
S² Works
Publisher
  • Japan King Records
Release Date
  • Japan December 4, 1998
Media Type Audio CD
Author Shiro Sagisu

S² Works is a collection of music arranged, produced, and mostly composed by Shiro Sagisu for the Neon Genesis Evangelion series and the Evangelion: Death and End of Evangelion movies. It was released on December 4, 1998, and comprises 7 CDs. The U.S. Imported version does not contain the 7th Bonus Disc. Its title is based on the S² Engine.

Contents

  • 1 Disc 1
  • 2 Disc 2
  • 3 Disc 3
  • 4 Disc 4
  • 5 Disc 5
  • 6 Disc 6
  • 7 Disc 7 (Bonus Disc)
  • 8 See also
  • 9 References

See also[edit]

Supplement to the S² Works Appendix includes listing of tracks not included in the S² Works.

References[edit]

  1. ↑ A truncated version of this piece appears on the soundtrack Neon Genesis Evangelion OST II.
  2. ↑ From Bach's "Suiten für Violoncello solo, Suite for Solo Cello No. 1 in G Major, BWV 1007
  3. ↑ This version was included on the Evangelion Death soundtrack.
  4. ↑ This version was included on the End of Evangelion soundtrack.
  5. ↑ Later included as a track on the 2003 soundtrack Refrain of Evangelion.
  6. ↑ From Dvořák's "Cello Concerto in B minor, Op. 104, B. 191 1. Allegro".

summary: S² Works is a collection of music arranged, produced, and mostly composed by Shiro Sagisu for the Neon Genesis Evangelion series and the Evangelion: Death and End of Evangelion movies. It was released on December 4, 1998, and comprises 7 CDs. The U.S. Imported version does not contain the 7th Bonus Disc. Its title is based on the S² Engine.

references.content: A truncated version of this piece appears on the soundtrack [[Neon Genesis Evangelion OST II]].
From Bach's "Suiten für Violoncello solo, Suite for Solo Cello No. 1 in G Major, BWV 1007
This version was included on the [[Evangelion Death]] soundtrack.
This version was included on the [[End of Evangelion (Soundtrack)|End of Evangelion]] soundtrack.
Later included as a track on the 2003 soundtrack ''[[Refrain of Evangelion]]''.
From Dvořák's "Cello Concerto in B minor, Op. 104, B. 191 1. Allegro".
